notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateSession, GetSession, UpdateSession, and DeleteSession are present in v65.110.0. There is no ListSessions family in generativeaiagentruntime.

Pinned OCI SDK identity and response shape: CreateSession requires agentEndpointId plus CreateSessionDetails and returns the Session body. GetSession, UpdateSession, and DeleteSession all require agentEndpointId plus sessionId in the request path. UpdateSession returns the Session body, and delete returns only headers.

Pinned provider coverage is unconfirmed. formal/sources.lock still points at github.com/oracle/terraform-provider-oci@eb653febb1bab4cc6650a96d404a8baf36fdf671, but the accessible provider inventory did not expose a checked-in Session resource or data source for generativeaiagentruntime. The providerResource name therefore stays a provisional OCI-style placeholder so generator-owned registry artifacts can derive stable links without claiming verified provider coverage.

Repo-authored runtime keeps path identity explicit and narrow: spec.agentEndpointId is required for create and reread, the runtime records bound agentEndpointId plus sessionId in status, and the published controller does not invent list-based bind or search semantics because the SDK exposes no list operation.

Repo-authored runtime treats create and update as synchronous read-after-write flows. The Session model exposes no lifecycle enum or service-local work-request helpers, so the reviewed runtime settles success from confirmed rereads and keeps delete confirmation on follow-up GetSession until OCI returns NotFound.
